annotations.label: Laplace operator (informal)

anchor.snippet: the Laplace operator or Laplacian is a differential operator given by the divergence of the gradient of a scalar function on Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib defines a `Laplacian` typeclass with notation `Δ`, instantiated on `E → F` for real inner product spaces via `InnerProductSpace.instLaplacian`.

annotations.label: Harmonic function

anchor.snippet: Solutions of Laplace's equation Δ f = 0 are called harmonic functions

annotations.note: Defined as being C^2 with `Δ f =ᶠ[𝓝 x] 0`; `HarmonicOnNhd` handles the on-a-set version.

annotations.label: Laplacian as divergence of gradient

anchor.snippet: defined as the divergence

annotations.note: Mathlib's Laplacian is defined via the second iterated derivative applied to the canonical covariant tensor, not literally as `div ∘ grad` — divergence is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Laplacian as sum of unmixed second partials

anchor.snippet: Explicitly, the Laplacian of f is thus the sum of all the unmixed second partial derivatives in the Cartesian coordinates

annotations.note: States `Δ f = fun x ↦ ∑ i, iteratedFDeriv ℝ 2 f x ![v i, v i]` for any orthonormal basis `v`.

annotations.label: Laplacian maps C^k to C^{k-2}

anchor.snippet: As a second-order differential operator, the Laplace operator maps C k functions to C k −2 functions for k ≥ 2

annotations.note: No lemma of the form `ContDiff k → ContDiff (k-2) (Δ f)` on the Laplacian was found.

annotations.label: Laplacian via sphere averages

anchor.snippet: Alternatively, the Laplace operator can be defined as

annotations.note: No definition of the Laplacian as a limit of the sphere-average deviation was found.

annotations.label: Negative semidefinite sign convention

anchor.snippet: is negative semidefinite

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Δ` uses the sum-of-second-partials sign (the negative-semidefinite convention), matching this Wikipedia convention.

annotations.label: Nonnegative sign convention

anchor.snippet: Another common convention inserts a minus sign and defines instead

annotations.note: No `-Δ` convention operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diffusion equilibrium and Laplace's equation

anchor.snippet: the net flux of u through the boundary

annotations.note: Physics motivation, not formalized.

annotations.label: Diffusion implies Laplace's equation

anchor.snippet: the entire equation Δ u = 0 is known as Laplace's equation

annotations.note: No formalization of a diffusion PDE / Fick-law derivation of Laplace's equation.

annotations.label: Average over a ball

anchor.snippet: the average value of

annotations.note: General measure-theoretic average is present, but a dedicated `ballAverage` for the Laplace/harmonic setting is not.

annotations.label: Average over a sphere

anchor.snippet: Similarly, the average value of

annotations.note: Circle (2D sphere) average is formalized; the higher-dimensional sphere-average operator specific to harmonic analysis is not.

annotations.label: Charge density from electrostatic potential

anchor.snippet: the charge distribution itself is given by the negative of the Laplacian

annotations.note: Physics application; no electrostatics API in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gauss's law derivation

anchor.snippet: This is a consequence of Gauss's law

annotations.note: Gauss's law is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mass distribution from gravitational potential

anchor.snippet: the negative of the Laplacian of the gravitational potential is the mass distribution

annotations.note: Physics application; not formalized.

annotations.label: Poisson's equation

anchor.snippet: Finding the potential function subject to suitable boundary conditions is equivalent to solving Poisson's equation

annotations.note: No `Poisson.equation` or analogous definition was found (the Poisson kernel/formula for harmonic functions exists, but not Poisson's PDE).

annotations.label: Harmonic functions minimize Dirichlet energy

anchor.snippet: solutions to Δ f = 0 in a region U are functions that make the Dirichlet energy functional stationary

annotations.note: Dirichlet energy is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirichlet stationary iff harmonic

anchor.snippet: Conversely, if E is stationary around f , then Δ f = 0

annotations.note: Dirichlet-energy variational characterization of harmonicity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplacian in 2D Cartesian coordinates

anchor.snippet: where x and y are the standard Cartesian coordinates of the xy -plane

annotations.note: Gives the 2D formula for `Δ f` on `ℂ` in the `(1, I)` basis, i.e. `∂²/∂x² + ∂²/∂y²`.

annotations.label: Laplacian in polar coordinates

anchor.snippet: where r represents the radial distance and θ the angle

annotations.note: No explicit polar-coordinate expression for the Laplacian was found.

annotations.label: Laplacian in 3D Cartesian coordinates

anchor.snippet: In Cartesian coordinates

annotations.note: General `n`-D Cartesian formula for `Δ f` via the standard orthonormal basis, which specialises to 3D.

annotations.label: Laplacian in cylindrical coordinates

anchor.snippet: represents the radial distance, φ the azimuth angle and z the height

annotations.note: No cylindrical-coordinate Laplacian formula was found.

annotations.label: Laplacian in spherical coordinates

anchor.snippet: where φ represents the azimuthal angle and θ the zenith angle

annotations.note: No spherical-coordinate Laplacian formula was found.

annotations.label: Laplacian in general curvilinear coordinates (3D)

anchor.snippet: In general curvilinear coordinates

annotations.note: No curvilinear-coordinate Laplacian formula was found.

annotations.label: Laplacian in N-dim curvilinear coordinates

anchor.snippet: In arbitrary curvilinear coordinates

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Radial/angular decomposition of Laplacian

anchor.snippet: the Laplacian decomposes into radial and angular parts

annotations.note: No radial/angular decomposition of the Laplacian is present.

annotations.label: Spherical harmonic eigenvalue equation

anchor.snippet: the angular factor must satisfy the eigenvalue equation

annotations.note: Spherical harmonics are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Radial equation and solid harmonics

anchor.snippet: gives the radial equation

annotations.note: Solid harmonics not formalized.

annotations.label: Radial harmonic functions on annulus

anchor.snippet: Thus every radial harmonic function on an annulus

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Spherical Laplacian via homogeneous extension

anchor.snippet: the spherical Laplacian of a function on

annotations.note: Spherical Laplacian and homogeneous-extension characterization not formalized.

annotations.label: Euclidean equivariance of Laplacian

anchor.snippet: The Laplacian is equivariant under pullback by every Euclidean transformation

annotations.note: No `laplacian_comp_isometry` / pullback-equivariance lemma was found; `laplacian_CLE_comp_left` covers left composition only.

annotations.label: Rotation/translation invariance in 2D

anchor.snippet: In two dimensions, this says that

annotations.note: Rotation/translation invariance of `Δ` is not stated.

annotations.label: Euclidean group invariance

anchor.snippet: is invariant under the natural action of the Euclidean group

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Characterization of Euclidean-invariant operators

anchor.snippet: those that commute with all Euclidean isometries are exactly the polynomial expressions in the Laplacian

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Angular Laplacian as Casimir

anchor.snippet: the angular part of the Euclidean Laplacian is, up to sign convention, the quadratic Casimir operator of the rotation group

annotations.note: No quadratic Casimir operator formalized; angular Laplacian not present.

annotations.label: Laplace–Beltrami from Casimir on homogeneous spaces

anchor.snippet: the Laplace–Beltrami operator is obtained from the quadratic Casimir of the acting Lie group

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Linearity of the Laplacian

anchor.snippet: The Laplace operator is linear

annotations.note: Additivity, negation, and scalar multiplication are proven under C^2 hypotheses (`laplacian_add`, `laplacian_neg`, `laplacian_smul`).

annotations.label: Ellipticity of the Laplacian

anchor.snippet: is an elliptic differential operator

annotations.note: No abstract notion of elliptic differential operator is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Green's identities for the Laplacian

anchor.snippet: Green's identities give

annotations.note: Green's identities are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Formal self-adjointness

anchor.snippet: the Laplacian is formally self-adjoint

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Energy identity

anchor.snippet: gives the energy identity

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Harmonic, subharmonic, superharmonic

anchor.snippet: is called harmonic if

annotations.note: Harmonic functions are formalized; subharmonic and superharmonic are not.

annotations.label: Mean value property

anchor.snippet: This is the mean value property for harmonic functions

annotations.note: The circle-average mean-value property is proven for real-valued harmonic functions on `ℂ`; the higher-dimensional sphere/ball version is not.

annotations.label: Maximum and minimum principles

anchor.snippet: A nonconstant harmonic function cannot attain an interior maximum or minimum

annotations.note: A maximum principle for harmonic (as opposed to holomorphic) functions was not found.

annotations.label: Elliptic regularity for the Laplacian

anchor.snippet: solutions of equations involving

annotations.note: General elliptic regularity is not formalized.

annotations.label: Smoothness/analyticity of harmonic functions

anchor.snippet: harmonic functions are smooth, and in fact real analytic

annotations.note: Proven for real-valued harmonic functions on `ℂ`; the general n-dimensional result is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weyl's lemma

anchor.snippet: Weyl's lemma states that if

annotations.note: Weyl's lemma (distributional harmonicity ⇒ smoothness) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplacian as Fourier multiplier

anchor.snippet: Thus the Laplacian is a Fourier multiplier with symbol

annotations.note: States `Δ f = -(2π)^2 • fourierMultiplierCLM F (‖·‖^2) f` on Schwartz functions; a distributional version is also proven.

annotations.label: Heat semigroup and fractional powers via Fourier

anchor.snippet: the heat semigroup corresponds to multiplication by

annotations.note: Heat semigroup and fractional Laplacian are not formalized.

annotations.label: Continuous spectrum on R^n

anchor.snippet: the spectrum of

annotations.note: Spectrum of `-Δ` on `L²(ℝⁿ)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Discrete spectrum on bounded domains

anchor.snippet: is a self-adjoint operator with compact resolvent

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Helmholtz equation

anchor.snippet: is the Helmholtz equation

annotations.note: No Helmholtz equation defined.

annotations.label: Spectrum of Laplace–Beltrami on compact manifold

anchor.snippet: the Laplace–Beltrami operator likewise has discrete nonnegative spectrum

annotations.note: Laplace–Beltrami on manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Fractional Laplacian (Fourier definition)

anchor.snippet: A nonlocal generalization of the Laplace operator is given by the fractional Laplacian

annotations.note: No fractional Laplacian is defined.

annotations.label: Fractional Laplacian (singular integral)

anchor.snippet: the fractional Laplacian can be defined by a singular integral

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Riesz potential

anchor.snippet: the Riesz potential of order

annotations.note: No Riesz potential is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riesz potential inverts fractional Laplacian

anchor.snippet: The inverse of the fractional Laplacian is closely related to the Riesz potential

annotations.note: Neither operator is formalized.

annotations.label: Bessel potential

anchor.snippet: the Bessel potential operator is defined by

annotations.note: Defined as the Fourier multiplier with symbol `(1 + ‖x‖²)^(s/2)` on tempered distributions.

annotations.label: Bessel potential spaces

anchor.snippet: The associated function spaces are the Bessel potential spaces

annotations.note: `MemSobolev s p f` is defined as `besselPotential s f ∈ Lp`, i.e. membership in a Bessel potential (Sobolev) space.

annotations.label: Vector Laplacian

anchor.snippet: The vector Laplace operator

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Δ` is defined for `f : E → F` with `F` an arbitrary normed vector space, so the vector-valued case is subsumed.

annotations.label: Vector Laplacian via Helmholtz decomposition

anchor.snippet: The vector Laplacian of a vector field

annotations.note: No `grad(div F) - curl(curl F)` decomposition or Helmholtz decomposition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector Laplacian in Cartesian coordinates

anchor.snippet: In Cartesian coordinates , this reduces to the much simpler expression

annotations.note: The general orthonormal-basis formula applied to vector-valued `f` gives the componentwise Cartesian form.

annotations.label: Tensor Laplacian

anchor.snippet: The Laplacian of any tensor field

annotations.note: Not formalized (no tensor-field Laplacian).

annotations.label: Navier–Stokes equation

anchor.snippet: An example of the usage of the vector Laplacian is the Navier-Stokes equations

annotations.note: Navier–Stokes equations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Wave equation for electric field

anchor.snippet: Another example is the wave equation for the electric field

annotations.note: Maxwell/wave equations for E are not formalized.

annotations.label: Heat semigroup

anchor.snippet: The Laplace operator generates the heat semigroup

annotations.note: Heat semigroup not formalized.

annotations.label: Smoothing effect of heat semigroup

anchor.snippet: A basic feature of the heat semigroup is its smoothing effect

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Heat kernel on manifolds

anchor.snippet: the same construction defines a heat semigroup whose integral kernel is again called the heat kernel

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplace–Beltrami operator

anchor.snippet: is the trace

annotations.note: No Laplace–Beltrami operator on Riemannian manifolds is defined.

annotations.label: Geometer's Laplacian

anchor.snippet: the "geometer's Laplacian" is expressed as

annotations.note: Not formalized.

annotations.label: Hodge Laplacian / Laplace–de Rham

anchor.snippet: the "Hodge" Laplacian is defined on differential forms

annotations.note: No Hodge/Laplace–de Rham operator on differential forms is defined.

annotations.label: Weitzenböck identity

anchor.snippet: This is known as the Laplace–de Rham operator , which is related to the Laplace–Beltrami operator by the Weitzenböck identity

annotations.note: Weitzenböck identity is not formalized.

annotations.label: D'Alembert operator

anchor.snippet: In Minkowski space the Laplace–Beltrami operator becomes the D'Alembert operator

annotations.note: D'Alembertian on Minkowski space is not defined in Mathlib.
